descriptions: Taghi organisation and Camorra are described as part of a super drug cartel together.

descriptions: Clan del Golfo protected Taghi's right-hand man, indicating cooperation.

descriptions: Noffel clan allied with Taghi organisation to assassinate Samir Bouyakhrichan.

Bishnoi Gang.description: Indian organized crime syndicate founded by Lawrence Bishnoi in the early 2010s. Based in Punjab, India, the gang is involved in extortion, drug trafficking, and targeted killings. It has been linked to over 800 sharpshooters worldwide and is designated a terrorist entity by Canada. The gang is known for high-profile murders including that of singer Sidhu Moose Wala.

descriptions: Bishnoi Gang killed a member of Khalistan Tiger Force.
edges.evidence_quote: Bishnoi's associates murdered Sukhdool Singh Gill, a member of the pro-Khalistani Khalistan Tiger Force, considered a terrorist organization by India.

descriptions: Bishnoi gang is listed as a rival of Brothers Keepers in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Red Scorpions , United Nations , Kang crime family(BIBO gang) , Bishnoi gang

One Million Boys.description: A robbery gang originally formed in Ajegunle, Lagos, Nigeria by about 20 boys to fight perceived injustice, but later hijacked into robbery, rape, and maiming. Known for notorious activities around Lagos and its surroundings. In 2012, about 130 suspected members were arrested by Lagos State Police.

descriptions: Top Crash Gang is an ally of Doddington per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Moss Side Bloods , Longsight Crew , Haydock Close Crew , Portuguese Mafia , Active Only , Top Crash Gang , Paul Massey (gangster)

Los Angeles crime family.description: Italian American Mafia crime family based in Los Angeles, California, founded around 1900 by Joseph Ardizzone. It gained power through bootlegging during Prohibition and peaked under Jack Dragna in the 1940s-1950s. The family has since declined due to RICO prosecutions and competition from other gangs, but remains active with an estimated 15-20 made members as of 2003.

Combat 18.description: Neo-Nazi terrorist organization founded in 1992 in the United Kingdom. Originally formed as a stewarding group for the British National Party, it later split and became an openly violent group. It has spread to other countries including Canada and Germany, and has been linked to murders of immigrants, non-whites, and dissidents. Banned in Canada and Germany, but still active.

descriptions: American Front's magazine had ties to Combat 18.
edges.evidence_quote: The Washington and California AF sections published The Voice of Revolution magazine, which had ties to Combat 18 in England.

descriptions: Infobox lists Combat 18 as an ally of Atomwaffen Division.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Combat 18

descriptions: An Atomwaffen affiliate was connected to Serbian Combat 18.
edges.evidence_quote: The man was discovered to be connected to Serbian Combat 18 and to another local neo-Nazi gang, " MC Srbi".

descriptions: Atomwaffen Division lists Combat 18 as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Combat 18

descriptions: Combat 18 is listed as an ally of Atomwaffen Division in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Combat 18

descriptions: Serbian Combat 18 trafficked firearms to Atomwaffen Division.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2019 Serbian Combat 18 was discovered to be trafficking firearms to Atomwaffen neo-Nazis selling handguns, assault rifles, grenades and RPG-7s from the Balkans to French neo-Nazis in Marseille.

descriptions: Blood & Honour is an affiliate of Combat 18.
edges.evidence_quote: On 21 June 2019, the government of Canada added Combat 18 (alongside its affiliate Blood & Honour) to its list of terrorist organisations

descriptions: BNP formed Combat 18 as a stewarding group.
edges.evidence_quote: In early 1992, the far-right British National Party (BNP) formed Combat 18 as a stewarding group

descriptions: Combat 18 was instrumental in setting up the Order of Nine Angles.
edges.evidence_quote: The New Zealand Police said in a statement that Combat 18 had been instrumental in setting up the O9A.

descriptions: Racial Volunteer Force split from Combat 18 but retains close links.
edges.evidence_quote: A group calling itself the Racial Volunteer Force split from C18 in 2002, although it has retained close links to its parent organisation.

descriptions: White Wolves are alleged to be a splinter group of Combat 18.
edges.evidence_quote: Some journalists believed that the White Wolves are a C18 splinter group, alleging that the group had been set up by Del O'Connor, the former second-in-command of C18

descriptions: Nazi Lowriders have ties with Combat 18.
edges.evidence_quote: Due to their extreme underground ties with other hardcore, racial organizations (such as Combat 18 or Blood and Honour)

Galloping Goose Motorcycle Club.description: Outlaw motorcycle club founded in 1942 in Los Angeles, California by Dick Hershberg. Originally an informal racing team, it became chartered in 1946 and expanded across the Midwestern US. Considered a second-tier outlaw motorcycle gang by law enforcement, it has been involved in violent crimes and has support clubs.

descriptions: El Forastero has close links to Galloping Goose MC, and they jointly pooled money to buy drugs for runs.
edges.evidence_quote: The club was founded in 1962 by Tom Fugle and Harlan "Tiny" Brower has chapters in Iowa, Minnesota, Kansas, and Missouri and close links to the Galloping Goose MC .

descriptions: Both clubs emerged as outlaw motorcycle clubs after the 1947 Hollister Rally.
edges.evidence_quote: This led to the beginning of the highly visible and structured 1% or outlaw motorcycle clubs , along with the Boozefighters MC when the AMA forbade club members to participate in AMA events unless they took off their patches.

descriptions: The text states a close relationship between the two clubs.
edges.evidence_quote: The club has a close relationship with El Forastero Motorcycle Club .

descriptions: Galloping Goose expanded into territory previously controlled by Pharaohs, indicating a conflict over territory.
edges.evidence_quote: An expert on outlaw motorcycle gangs from Missouri State Highway Patrol said the Galloping Goose were expanding into territory formerly controlled by the Pharaohs motorcycle club during the 1980s and 1990s.

descriptions: Vieux-Doo Dawgs M.C. is a support club created by Galloping Goose.
edges.evidence_quote: He described them as a "one percenter club", which created their first support club name "Vieux-Doo Dawgs M.C." This was established in Louisiana dated 1998.

descriptions: Galloping Goose is listed as an allied smaller motorcycle gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The Hells Angels are allied with numerous smaller motorcycle gangs – such as the ... Galloping Goose

edges.evidence_quote: On August 13, 2011, law enforcement authorities reported that the Vagos Motorcycle Club and the Galloping Goose Motorcycle Club were involved in a shootout which shut down traffic on I-44 near Waynesville, Missouri.

descriptions: The Komarovskaya OPG receives strong support from the Usvyatsov-Putyrsky gang for cocaine supply and counterfeit dollar trade.
edges.evidence_quote: Komarovskaya OPG steal automobiles, commit robberies, provide protection racketeering, and receive strong support from the Usvyatsov-Putyrsky gang and its AOZT "Putus" to organize the supply of cocaine from South America into Russia, Finland, Scandinavia and Europe and the trade in counterfeit dollars.

descriptions: The Usvyatsov-Putyrsky gang works closely with the Komarovskaya organized criminal group.
edges.evidence_quote: The Usvyatsov-Putyrsky gang (AOZT "Putus") ... works closely with the Komarovskaya organized criminal group.

Weather Underground.description: American Marxist militant organization active from 1969 to 1977, originally a faction of Students for a Democratic Society. It sought to overthrow the U.S. government through a bombing campaign targeting government buildings and banks, often issuing evacuation warnings. The group was classified as a domestic terrorist group by the FBI and disintegrated after the Vietnam War peace accord.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Historical: , Black Liberation Army , Symbionese Liberation Army , Weather Underground

descriptions: The Weather Underground sympathized with and supported the Black Panther Party.
edges.evidence_quote: The Weathermen strongly sympathized with the radical Black Panther Party; the police killing of Panther Fred Hampton prompted the Weathermen to issue a declaration of war upon the U.S. government.

descriptions: Weather Underground carried out actions in solidarity with Black Panther Party members.

Clan Sacco-Bocchetti.description: The Clan Sacco-Bocchetti is a Camorra organization active in the northeastern suburbs of Naples, specifically in the San Pietro a Patierno and Secondigliano districts. It is named after Gennaro Sacco and Gaetano Bocchetti, the latter a founder of the Alleanza di Secondigliano. The clan has been involved in drug trafficking and extortion, with several members arrested or killed in recent years.

descriptions: The Sacco-Bocchetti clan is a subgroup of the Licciardi clan, which is a Camorra clan.
edges.evidence_quote: The Sacco-Bocchetti clan is a subgroup of the Licciardi clan

descriptions: Gaetano Bocchetti, co-founder of the clan, was the founder of the Alleanza di Secondigliano, indicating a structural tie.
edges.evidence_quote: Il clan prende il nome da Gennaro Sacco e Gaetano Bocchetti , quest'ultimo fautore dell' Alleanza di Secondigliano .

descriptions: Sacco-Bocchetti clan is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Scissionisti di Secondigliano, Ruocco clan, Abbinante clan, Sacco-Bocchetti clan

descriptions: The Sacco-Bocchetti clan is a subgroup of the Licciardi clan.
edges.evidence_quote: The Sacco-Bocchetti clan is a subgroup of the Licciardi clan

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Russo clan , Alfieri clan (defunct) , Licciardi clan , Galasso clan (defunct) , Sacco-Bocchetti clan , Contini clan

descriptions: The EPB worked in conjunction with FARP.
edges.evidence_quote: In September 1979, the EPB revealed that it was working in conjunction with the Volunteers, FARP and FALN.

descriptions: The EPB joined the FARP and the Volunteers in planting bombs.
edges.evidence_quote: On October 28, 1986, they joined the FARP and the Volunteers in planting eight bombs in militarily sites across Puerto Rico.

Purple Gang.description: The Purple Gang was a Jewish criminal mob of bootleggers and hijackers based in Detroit, Michigan, during the Prohibition era. Founded in the 1910s, it became Detroit's dominant criminal gang through bootlegging, armed robbery, and extortion. Excessive violence and infighting led to its collapse in the 1930s.

descriptions: The American Mafia is allied with the Purple Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Sicilian Mafia , Camorra , 'Ndrangheta , various independent Italian-American gangs (such as the 10th & Oregon Crew , Purple Gang , and Tanglewood Boys )

descriptions: The Purple Gang supplied Canadian whisky to the Chicago Outfit under a business accommodation.
edges.evidence_quote: Chicago gangster Al Capone was against expanding his rackets in Detroit, so he began a business accommodation with the Purple Gang in order to prevent a bloody war. For several years, the gang managed the prosperous business of supplying Canadian whisky to the Capone organization in Chicago.

descriptions: MLAZ/FURNAC was reported to cooperate with RCD-Goma.
edges.evidence_quote: A number of smaller Mai-Mai groups, such as the Mudundu 40 / Front de Résistance et de Défense du Kivu (FRDKI) and Mouvement de Lutte contre l'Agression au Zaïre / Forces Unies de Résistance Nationale contre l'Agression de la Républíque Démocratique du Congo (MLAZ/FURNAC), were reported to cooperate with the Rwandan military and Rally for Congolese Democracy–Goma (RCD-Goma).

descriptions: The Trafficante family (as part of Antinori's organization) was rival to Wall's organization.
edges.evidence_quote: Wall controlled his organization from the Tampa neighborhood of Ybor City, and employed Italians, Cubans and men of other ethnicities in his organization. His only rival for criminal rackets in the Tampa Bay area was Italian Mafia boss Ignacio Antinori.

descriptions: Morabito and Zavettieri were arrested together in the Onorata Sanità operation, indicating association.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 28 gennaio 2008, nell'operazione Onorata Sanità in Calabria, vengono tenute sotto custodia cautelare 18 persone tra cui il consigliere regionale "Domenico Crea" e esponenti della cosca dei Morabito, dei Zavettieri e dei Cordì

Lost Breed Motorcycle Club.description: Outlaw motorcycle club based in Nelson, New Zealand, founded in 1976. The club claimed opposition to methamphetamine and domestic violence, though police viewed these claims skeptically. It was shut down in December 2015 when members patched over to the Hells Angels.

descriptions: Lost Breed merged into Hells Angels, indicating a structural tie.
edges.evidence_quote: The Hells Angels 'patched over' the Lost Breed in Nelson in 2016

descriptions: Highway 61 and Lost Breed clashed at the 1979 Nelson Mardi Gras, resulting in injuries and arrests.
edges.evidence_quote: At the 1979 Nelson Mardi Gras event the Lost Breed clashed with members of Highway 61 from Wellington.

descriptions: Lone Legion is a member of the A-Team alliance with Lost Breed MC.
edges.evidence_quote: According to media and Police the club is a member of the "A-Team", an alliance between several NZ biker groups including the Outcasts MC, the Epitaph Riders MC, the Forty-Fives MC, the Southern Vikings MC, Satan's Slaves MC, Sinn Fein MC and the Lost Breed MC .

descriptions: Fourth Reich is listed as a rival of Lost Breed.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Fourth Reich (New Zealand gang)

descriptions: Hells Angels shut down Lost Breed and absorbed some members.
edges.evidence_quote: At the end of 2015, the group was "shut down" by the Hells Angels with some members patching over to a Hells Angels chapter established in the city.

descriptions: Os Caneos are in a feud with the Charlín clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Os Caneos ... are involved in a feud with the Charlín clan.

descriptions: Los Charlines are rivals with Os Caneos.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Os Caneos

descriptions: Escobar hired ELN for bombings and assassinations.
edges.evidence_quote: The ELN had relations with Pablo Escobar, with Escobar often hiring the ELN to carry out bombings and assassinations against government targets.

descriptions: CENTRAL is an affiliated organization of Manitoba Warriors.
edges.evidence_quote: Saskatchewan Warriors, Alberta Warriors, CENTRAL, Loyalty Honour Silence and MOB Squad are some of their more well known affiliations.

Amber Mafia.description: Criminal network in north-west Ukraine involved in illegal amber mining on government land, smuggling it out for black market sale with the aid of armed gangs and corrupt politicians. Estimated to involve around 10,000 people, with proceeds potentially covering Ukraine's military expenditures. Active since at least 2016.
